title: How to Download LIC Policy Bond Online (Duplicate Copy 2026)
summary: Lost your LIC policy bond? Get a duplicate copy online or offline. Step-by-step process, documents required, fees, and timeline for 2026.
content:
## How to Download Duplicate LIC Policy Bond 2026

### Online + Branch Process

1. Log in to LIC Customer Portal (licindia.in)
2. Go to Service Requests > Duplicate Policy Bond
3. Upload ID proof and pay fee (Rs. 100-200)
4. Visit home branch with original ID proof
5. Complete indemnity bond (Form 3815)
6. Collect duplicate bond in 15-30 days

### Offline Process

1. Visit home branch
2. Fill Form 3815 (Indemnity Bond on stamp paper)
3. Submit ID proof + 2 passport photos
4. Pay Rs. 200-400 fee
5. Collect in 15-45 days

### If You Do Not Remember Policy Number

LIC can trace your policy using:
- Full name and date of birth
- Fathers/husbands name
- PAN number
- Premium payment records

### Tips to Protect Your Bond

- Take a photo and save on phone + cloud
- Upload to DigiLocker
- Keep original in fireproof safe or bank locker
- Inform nominee about bond location

title: No Claim Bonus in Car Insurance: Complete Guide 2026
summary: Everything about No Claim Bonus (NCB) in car insurance -- how it works, discount rates, how to transfer, NCB protection cover, and what happens when you make a claim.
content:
## No Claim Bonus in Car Insurance 2026

### NCB Discount Slab

| Claim-Free Years | Discount |
|---|---|
| 1 year | 20% |
| 2 years | 25% |
| 3 years | 35% |
| 4 years | 45% |
| 5+ years | 50% |

### Key Points

- NCB applies only to own-damage premium (not third-party)
- Resets to 0% after any claim
- Portable when switching insurers
- Belongs to policyholder, not the car
- 90-day grace period to retain NCB after renewal date

### When to Pay Out of Pocket vs Claim

If repair cost < NCB discount amount, pay out of pocket to preserve NCB.

**Example:** NCB saves Rs. 10,000. Repair costs Rs. 5,000. Pay out of pocket.

### NCB Protection Add-on

Costs Rs. 1,000-2,000 extra. Allows 1-2 claims per year without losing NCB. Worth it if you have 35%+ NCB.

### Common Myths

- NCB is lost if you do not renew on time (FALSE -- 90-day grace period)
- NCB applies to third-party premium (FALSE -- only own-damage)
- NCB can be transferred to spouse (FALSE -- stays with policyholder)

title: How to Surrender LIC Policy Online 2026: Process, Charges & Alternatives
summary: Thinking of surrendering your LIC policy? Learn the online process, surrender value calculation, charges, tax implications, and better alternatives in 2026.
content:
## How to Surrender LIC Policy Online 2026

### When Can You Surrender?

After 3 full years of premiums paid (policy must have surrender value).

### Surrender Value Calculation

| Policy Year | Surrender Value Factor |
|---|---|
| Year 3 | 30% of premiums paid |
| Year 4-5 | 50% |
| Year 6-7 | 65% |
| Year 8-9 | 75% |
| Year 10+ | 90% + bonuses |

**Example:** Rs. 50,000/year premium, paid for 5 years = Rs. 2,50,000. Surrender value at 50% = Rs. 1,25,000 (you lose Rs. 1,25,000).

### Online Process

1. Log in to LIC Customer Portal
2. Service Requests > Policy Surrender
3. Visit home branch with original bond
4. Receive payment in 7-15 days via NEFT

### Tax Implications

- Premium above Rs. 2,50,000/year: Surrender value is taxable
- Premium below Rs. 2,50,000: Tax-exempt under Section 10(10D)
- TDS of 5% if surrender value exceeds Rs. 1 lakh

### Better Alternatives

1. **Make policy paid-up** -- stop premiums, keep reduced cover
2. **Policy loan** -- borrow up to 90% of surrender value at 9-10.5%
3. **Revival** -- if lapsed, revive instead of surrendering

**Talk to our experts before surrendering:** [Free consultation](https://insurancesupport.online/contact).

title: Does Health Insurance Cover Ambulance Charges in India? (2026 Guide)
summary: Most health insurance plans in India cover ambulance charges for emergency hospitalization. Learn about coverage limits and how to claim.
content:
Medical emergencies strike without warning. One of the first things during a health crisis is reaching the hospital quickly - usually by ambulance. But ambulance services in India cost Rs.1,500-15,000.

Does your health insurance cover ambulance charges?

The short answer is yes - most comprehensive health insurance plans do cover ambulance expenses. But coverage amounts, types of services, and eligibility criteria vary.

Types of Ambulance Covered:
- Road Ambulance: Covered under almost all plans
- Air Ambulance: Available in select premium plans
- Train Ambulance: Some insurers cover this

Conditions:
1. Emergency Only - genuine medical emergencies
2. Network Hospital - transport to nearest network hospital
3. Pre-Authorization - some plans require this

How to Claim:
- Cashless: Settled directly at network hospitals
- Reimbursement: Submit receipt, admission papers, claim form, policy copy, FIR (for accidents)

Pro Tips:
- Always keep the original ambulance receipt
- Call your insurer first if the situation allows
- Check your policy wording for limits

Need help choosing health insurance? Contact Insurance Support for expert advice.

title: Claim Intimation in Health Insurance: Complete Guide (2026)
summary: Claim intimation is the first and most crucial step in the health insurance claim process. Learn why timely intimation matters.
content:
When a medical emergency strikes, the last thing on your mind is paperwork. But claim intimation - informing your insurer about a claim - is critical.

What Is Claim Intimation?
It is the process of informing your health insurance company that you are seeking medical treatment and intend to file a claim.

Why Timely Intimation Matters:
1. Cashless claims require pre-authorization
2. Avoid claim rejection - insurers require intimation within 24-48 hours for emergencies
3. Faster processing

Timelines:
- Emergency Hospitalization: Within 24-48 hours
- Planned Hospitalization: 4-7 days before admission
- Day Care Procedures: 24 hours before or after
- Accident Cases: Within 24 hours

How to Intimate:
1. Toll-Free Helpline - fastest method
2. Email to claims department
3. Mobile App
4. Through hospital insurance desk

If You Miss the Deadline:
Contact your insurer immediately with a valid reason. Submit a written explanation with supporting medical records.

Need help with a claim issue? Contact Insurance Support for expert assistance.

title: HRA Exemption: How to Save Tax on House Rent (2026)
summary: House Rent Allowance (HRA) is a valuable tax-saving tool. Learn how HRA exemption is calculated and maximized.
content:
If you are a salaried employee who pays rent, HRA is one of the most valuable tax-saving tools available.

What Is HRA Exemption?
HRA exemption is the portion exempt from income tax under Section 10(13A). It is the MINIMUM of:
1. Actual HRA received
2. Rent paid minus 10% of basic salary
3. 50% of basic (metro) or 40% (non-metro)

Example:
- Basic: Rs.50,000/month, HRA: Rs.20,000/month, Rent: Rs.15,000/month, City: Mumbai
- Actual HRA: Rs.2,40,000
- Rent - 10% basic: Rs.1,80,000 - Rs.60,000 = Rs.1,20,000
- 50% of basic: Rs.3,00,000
- HRA Exemption = Rs.1,20,000

Documents Required:
- Rent receipts (with landlords PAN if rent exceeds Rs.1 lakh/year)
- Rent agreement
- Form 16 from employer

Tips to Maximize:
1. Negotiate HRA in your salary structure
2. Pay rent to family members
3. Always collect rent receipts
4. Get landlords PAN for rent above Rs.1 lakh/year

Need help with tax planning? Contact Insurance Support for expert advice.

title: No Claim Bonus (NCB) in Car Insurance: Complete Guide (2026)
summary: No Claim Bonus can save you up to 50% on your car insurance premium. Learn how NCB works and how to protect it.
content:
No Claim Bonus (NCB) can save you up to 50% on your car insurance premium. Yet many car owners lose it unnecessarily.

What Is NCB?
NCB is a discount on your own damage premium for not making claims during a policy year.

NCB Slabs:
- 1 claim-free year: 20%
- 2 years: 25%
- 3 years: 35%
- 4 years: 45%
- 5+ years: 50%

How to Protect Your NCB:
1. Do not file small claims - if claim amount is less than NCB discount, pay out of pocket
2. Renew on time - even a one-day lapse resets NCB
3. Port your policy - NCB transfers when switching insurers
4. Buy NCB protection add-on

When You Sell Your Car:
NCB is attached to the policyholder, not the car. Get an NCB retention certificate from your insurer.

Need help with car insurance? Contact Insurance Support to compare quotes and maximize your NCB.

title: Critical Illness Insurance: Why You Need It (2026)
summary: Critical illness insurance provides a lump sum payout on diagnosis of serious conditions. Learn why it is essential and how to choose.
content:
A cancer diagnosis, heart attack, or stroke are not just medical emergencies - they are financial ones. Treatment costs range from Rs.5-50 lakhs or more.

What Is Critical Illness Insurance?
It pays a LUMP SUM amount on diagnosis of specified critical illnesses. Unlike regular health insurance, it gives you a fixed amount regardless of actual expenses.

Covered Illnesses:
Most plans cover 10-20 conditions including cancer, heart attack, stroke, kidney failure, major organ transplant, and paralysis.

Standalone vs Rider:
- Standalone: Separate policy, higher sum insured (Rs.10-50L)
- Rider: Attached to base policy, lower sum insured (Rs.5-15L)

How Much Cover?
Aim for 3-5 times your annual income.

Key Conditions:
- Waiting Period: 90 days from policy start
- Survival Period: 15-30 days after diagnosis

Need help choosing critical illness cover? Contact Insurance Support to compare plans.

title: Senior Citizen Health Insurance: Complete Guide (2026)
summary: Health insurance for senior citizens is more important than ever. Learn about the best plans and how to manage premiums.
content:
If your parents are above 60, you know the challenge: high premiums, exclusions, and restrictive terms. Yet health insurance for seniors is essential.

Why Seniors Need Insurance:
- Healthcare costs rise with age
- Higher hospitalization risk
- Protect retirement savings
- Tax benefits under Section 80D (up to Rs.50,000)

Challenges:
- High premiums: Rs.25,000-60,000/year for Rs.5-10L cover
- Pre-existing condition waiting periods: 2-4 years
- Co-pay: 10-20% in many plans
- Limited sum insured: Rs.10-25L

Best Practices:
1. Buy before 60 if possible
2. Consider super top-up plans
3. Look for low co-pay plans
4. Check network hospital coverage
5. Maintain continuous coverage

Need help finding the right plan for your parents? Contact Insurance Support.

title: Term Insurance vs Endowment Plan: Which Is Better? (2026)
summary: Term insurance provides pure life cover at low cost. Endowment plans combine insurance with savings. We compare both.
content:
Choosing between term insurance and endowment plans is one of the most important financial decisions you will make.

Term Insurance:
- Lowest premiums (Rs.1Cr cover for Rs.800-1,500/month)
- High coverage, easy to understand
- No maturity benefit - pure risk cover

Endowment Plan:
- Combines insurance with savings
- 5-10x higher premiums for same cover
- Returns: 4-6% per year
- Guaranteed maturity benefit

Comparison:
| Feature | Term Insurance | Endowment |
|---|---|---|---|
| Premium (Rs.1Cr) | Rs.12,000-18,000/yr | Rs.80,000-1,50,000/yr |
| Death Benefit | Rs.1 crore | Rs.1 crore + bonus |
| Maturity | Nil | Sum assured + bonus |

The Verdict:
Buy TERM insurance for life cover + invest the difference in MUTUAL FUNDS. This gives higher coverage AND better returns.

Need help choosing? Contact Insurance Support to compare plans.

title: Third Party vs Comprehensive Car Insurance: Which to Buy?
summary: Third party insurance is legally mandatory. But is it enough? We compare both types.
content:
Every vehicle owner must have third party motor insurance. But is it enough?

Third Party Insurance covers:
- Third party death/injury and property damage
Does NOT cover:
- Damage to your own car, your injuries, theft, natural disasters

Comprehensive Insurance covers:
- Third party liability + own damage + personal accident cover

Scenarios:
- Your car damaged: Third party = you pay. Comprehensive = insurer pays.
- Car stolen: Third party = nothing. Comprehensive = insurer pays IDV.
- Flood damage: Third party = you pay. Comprehensive = insurer covers.

Premium (Third Party, fixed by IRDAI):
- Up to 1000cc: Rs.2,097/year
- 1000-1500cc: Rs.3,416/year
- Above 1500cc: Rs.7,897/year

The Bottom Line:
For most car owners, comprehensive insurance is worth the extra premium. One major accident can exceed years of premium payments.

Need help? Contact Insurance Support to compare plans.

title: ULIP vs Mutual Fund: Which Is Better Investment? (2026)
summary: ULIPs combine insurance with market-linked returns. Mutual funds are pure investments. We compare both.
content:
ULIPs and mutual funds are both market-linked, but serve different purposes.

ULIP:
- Insurance + investment in one product
- Tax benefits (Section 80C, 10(10D))
- High charges: 3-6% annually
- Lock-in: 5 years
- Returns: 6-10%

Mutual Fund:
- Higher returns: 8-14%
- Lower charges: 0.5-2.5%
- No lock-in (except ELSS: 3 years)
- No life cover
- LTCG tax applies

The Verdict:
Buy TERM insurance for life cover + MUTUAL FUNDS for wealth creation. Better coverage AND higher returns than ULIP.

Need help with investment strategy? Contact Insurance Support.

title: Travel Insurance for International Trips: Complete Guide (2026)
summary: Planning an international trip? Learn what travel insurance covers, how much it costs, and why it is essential.
content:
Before you jet off on your international adventure, there is one more thing you need: travel insurance.

Why Travel Insurance?
A medical emergency abroad can cost lakhs - sometimes tens of lakhs. Travel insurance protects from:
- Medical emergencies and evacuation
- Trip cancellation
- Baggage loss/delay
- Flight delays
- Personal liability
- Passport loss

Cost:
| Duration | Asia | Europe | USA/Canada |
|---|---|---|---|
| 7-15 days | Rs.300-600 | Rs.800-2,000 | Rs.1,200-3,000 |

Schengen Requirement:
Travel insurance is MANDATORY for Schengen visa. Minimum: EUR 30,000 medical coverage.

What to Look For:
- Medical coverage: Rs.5-50L depending on destination
- Emergency evacuation
- Trip cancellation: Rs.50,000+
- Baggage cover: Rs.25,000+
- 24/7 assistance helpline

Need help choosing travel insurance? Contact Insurance Support.

title: IRDAI Rules on Claim Rejection: Your Rights (2026)
summary: Know your rights when an insurance claim is rejected. IRDAI has strict rules on when insurers can reject claims.
content:
Few things are more frustrating than having your insurance claim rejected. But IRDAI has strict rules.

Valid Reasons for Rejection:
- Non-disclosure of pre-existing conditions
- Waiting period not served
- Excluded conditions
- Lapsed policy
- Fraud

Invalid Reasons:
- Non-disclosure of unrelated conditions
- Late intimation with valid reason
- Minor documentation issues

What If Rejected?
1. Get rejection in writing
2. File grievance with insurers GRO
3. Escalate to IRDAI (igms.irdai.gov.in, 155255)
4. Approach Insurance Ombudsman
5. Consumer Court

Key IRDAI Protections:
- 30-day settlement deadline
- No rejection for non-material non-disclosure
- Portability rights
- 15-30 day free look period

Has your claim been rejected? Contact Insurance Support for expert assistance.

title: Best Time to Buy Health Insurance: Why Earlier Is Better
summary: The best time to buy health insurance is when you are young and healthy. We explain why buying early saves money.
content:
Many young professionals delay buying health insurance because they are healthy. But the best time to buy is exactly when you think you do not need it.

Why Buy Early?

1. Lower Premiums:
- Age 25: Rs.6,000-8,000 for Rs.10L cover
- Age 45: Rs.12,000-20,000 for same cover
- Age 65: Rs.30,000-60,000

2. Shorter waiting periods - pre-existing disease waiting periods pass while you are healthy
3. No medical tests - not required below 45 for most plans
4. Better coverage options available
5. Accumulate NCB faster
6. Avoid rejection - lock in coverage before conditions develop

The Cost of Waiting:
Buying at 25 vs 45 means lower premiums, full waiting period credit, 50% NCB, and no exclusions.

The best time to buy was yesterday. The second best time is today.

Ready to buy? Contact Insurance Support to compare plans.
